definition: The sovereign-corporate credit nexus is tightening through multiple transmission channels: fiscal deficit creating structural Treasury yield floors, TCJA extension tax policy uncertainty, and European doom loop 2.0 dynamics (France deficit ~5.5% GDP, debt >110% GDP, bank-sovereign linkage). Direction is clear and mechanisms are measurable, but may not be mispriced.
evidence: Fiscal deficit creating structural Treasury yield floor
TCJA extension uncertainty affecting corporate tax planning
France deficit ~5.5% GDP, debt >110% GDP with bank-sovereign linkage
Pair_3 noted risks may be present but not necessarily mispriced
open_questions: Are sovereign-corporate nexus risks mispriced or merely present and priced?
How does European doom loop 2.0 transmit to US corporate credit?
What is probability of TCJA non-extension and its credit impact?
